message:
fcp-postgresql: real-Postgres testcontainer transaction harness

The existing wiremock tests prove request shapes but not transaction
semantics — a connector that accidentally double-began on the same
session, leaked pinned backend state across an error path, or
confused commit with rollback would still pass them.

Add a feature-gated `integration-testcontainer` test harness in
`tests/transaction_integration.rs`. Boots a real `postgres:15-alpine`
testcontainer, then stands up a minimal axum HTTP shim that
implements the bespoke `POST /rest/v1/rpc/transaction` RPC the
PostgresClient speaks against PostgREST. The shim translates
`{action: begin|commit|rollback, ...}` into real `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` /
`ROLLBACK` SQL on per-txn pinned `tokio_postgres` connections — the
same semantics a real PostgREST RPC binding would have. (Booting a
full PostgREST container plus the custom RPC function it would need
is an order of magnitude more wiring; this shim covers the contract
the connector actually relies on.)

Four invariant tests:

  - `transaction_commit_makes_writes_visible_and_releases_session`:
    BEGIN → INSERT → COMMIT visible on a fresh connection AND the
    pinned session is released from the shim's active map.
  - `transaction_rollback_discards_writes_and_releases_session`:
    BEGIN → INSERT → ROLLBACK NOT visible AND pinned session
    released.
  - `concurrent_begins_produce_distinct_pinned_sessions`: two
    overlapping `transaction_begin` calls return distinct txn_ids,
    each with its own pinned PG client. Commit on one + rollback on
    the other prove the sessions are independent — A's row survives,
    B's row does not. Closes the bead's "one connector session
    cannot fan out multiple open transactions or leak pinned backend
    connections" requirement.
  - `commit_with_unknown_txn_id_does_not_leak_sessions`: a bogus
    commit returns 4xx and does NOT mutate the active-session map;
    a previously-opened real txn stays alive and can still be
    rolled back cleanly.

Cargo.toml gains an `integration-testcontainer` feature plus four
optional deps (`testcontainers`, `tokio`, `tokio-postgres`, `axum`).
The optional deps live under `[dependencies]` rather than
`[dev-dependencies]` because Cargo disallows `optional = true` in
`[dev-dependencies]`; the feature gate keeps default builds
offline-friendly and docker-less. Default `cargo test -p fcp-postgresql`
still runs 187/187 wiremock tests with zero infrastructure changes.

Verified:
  cargo test -p fcp-postgresql --features integration-testcontainer \\
      --test transaction_integration
  → 4/4 pass against real Postgres in ~12s

Closes flywheel_connectors-x6qrn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -34,5 +34,21 @@ thiserror.workspace = true
 tracing.workspace = true
 tracing-subscriber.workspace = true
 
+# Integration-testcontainer deps — only pulled in when the
+# `integration-testcontainer` feature is enabled. Kept in
+# `[dependencies]` (not `[dev-dependencies]`) because Cargo disallows
+# `optional = true` on dev-dependencies; they are feature-gated so
+# default builds stay offline-friendly and docker-less.
+testcontainers = { version = "0.23", optional = true }
+tokio = { workspace = true, optional = true }
+tokio-postgres = { version = "0.7", optional = true }
+axum = { version = "0.7", optional = true }
+
 [dev-dependencies]
 wiremock.workspace = true
+
+[features]
+default = []
+# Enable real-Postgres testcontainer integration tests. Requires a
+# running Docker daemon. See tests/transaction_integration.rs.
+integration-testcontainer = ["dep:testcontainers", "dep:tokio", "dep:tokio-postgres", "dep:axum"]
